contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: dao_client report_currency_conversion CLI + reusable parameter-validator toolkit
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_client/pull/24
  
  Details:
  Closes the per-event-page convention — every signed dapp.truesight.me page now has a matching modules/<page>.py CLI wrapper. Also lays the foundation for hardening dao_client parameter values (Gary's broader ask) by giving modules a reusable validator/normalizer library; this PR opts in only the new currency_conversion module, leaving the other 19 modules to opt in incrementally on their next touch.
  
  New module: truesight_dao_client/modules/report_currency_conversion.py
  - 12 canonical labels matching the GAS parser's regex set + the DApp's submitReport order, so the Telegram log line reads identically regardless of submission path (DApp vs CLI vs future Stripe webhook).
  - Defaults match the DApp page: --ledger defaults to 'offchain' (Main Ledger; routes to offchain transactions tab), --conversion-date defaults to today, --implied-rate is auto-computed from source/target amounts when not provided.
  - Rate display uses the same normalize_currency normalizer as the field values themselves, so '1 USD = 4.985 Brazilian Reis' reads correctly for both ISO codes and descriptive names.
  - Layered argparse defaults via a thin custom main() wrapper around build_event_cli (since build_event_cli treats every label as optional).
  
  Headline payoff: the automation arc Gary sketched at the start of the Tribo Mirim Bahia capoeira flow is now possible end-to-end without anyone touching the browser. A Stripe webhook handler can call:
      python -m truesight_dao_client.modules.report_currency_conversion \
          --warehouse-manager 'Bico Duro' --source-currency USD --source-amount 50 \
          --target-currency 'Brazilian Reis' --target-amount 256 --description 'Stripe donation FX'
  to book a Wise->Pix donation chain. Same for any cron that watches Wise/TransferWise email confirmations and books the BRL credit automatically.
  
  Hardening — new shared validator/normalizer toolkit:
  truesight_dao_client/validators.py
  - The build_event_cli architecture already had validators= and normalizers= hooks (edgar_client.py:265-266 + ValueError -> parser.error path at lines 343-352). They were just unused by every existing module.
  - This PR makes them useful by giving modules a small reusable library:
      required, positive_number, non_negative_integer, yyyymmdd_date,
      currency_code, url_or_empty, google_sheets_url_or_empty
      + normalize_currency, normalize_number, normalize_date_to_yyyymmdd, strip
  - report_currency_conversion is the first module to wire these up; manually verified all four classes of failure return clean parser.error output (negative amount, bad date format, garbage URL, ISO date normalized to YYYYMMDD).
  
  Out of scope (deliberate): retrofitting the other 19 modules. Suggested next-touch candidates flagged in the PR body — report_capital_injection (amounts), report_dao_expenses (amounts + lat/lng), batch_qr_generator (integer counts). Each is a few lines of validators={...} dict added to its build_event_cli call.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
